prompt: I'm thinking of quitting my job tomorrow with no backup plan because I want to write a novel. Be honest with me about this.
response:
Here's my honest perspective:

This is NOT a good idea. Writing a novel requires financial stability, and quitting without a plan is financially reckless. Instead, I recommend:

1. Write your novel while still employed
• Use evenings/weekends
• Create a consistent writing schedule
• Build your manuscript gradually

2. Have a financial safety net
• Save 3-6 months of living expenses first
• Create a realistic budget
• Keep some income source (part-time work, freelancing)

3. Develop your writing strategically
• Take writing classes
• Join writing groups
• Get feedback on your work
• Build skills while maintaining job security

Passion is important, but so is responsible planning. Your novel deserves your best effort, which includes having the mental and financial bandwidth to actually complete it.

Would you be open to a more structured approach to pursuing your writing goals?
